source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 14.4.2 repository configuration (pp. 268-269)

questions.q: Where are the repository configuration files stored, and what is their extension?
questions.why: Repo definitions live in /etc/yum.repos.d/ as .repo files; each may hold one or more repositories.

questions.q: In a .repo definition, what is the text inside the square brackets, e.g. [baseos]?
options: The repository ID, which must be unique
questions.why: The [ ] holds the repository ID (must be unique); the 'name' option gives the descriptive name.

questions.q: What does the 'enabled' option control, and what is its default?
options: Whether the repo is used as a package source; default is 1 (True)
Whether GPG checking is on; default 0
Whether the repo is a mirror; default 1
questions.why: enabled=1 (the default) includes the repo as a source; set 0 to disable it.

questions.q: What does 'gpgcheck' do, and what is its default value?
options: Whether to GPG-signature-check packages from the repo; default is 0 (False)
Whether to enable the repo; default 1
questions.why: gpgcheck toggles signature checking (default 0/False); the gpgkey option gives the public key path.

questions.q: What is the difference between the 'baseurl' and 'mirrorlist' options?
options: baseurl is a direct list of repo URLs; mirrorlist is a URL that returns a list of mirrors
baseurl is for GPG keys; mirrorlist is for packages
baseurl disables the repo; mirrorlist enables it
questions.why: baseurl points straight at the repo URL(s); mirrorlist points at a URL that hands back a list of mirrors (both support https/http/ftp/file/NFS and $variables).
